artPrompt: A sacrificial bargain at the midway: a small relic crumbling into hot-gold sparks above a barker's outstretched palm, a sign promising nothing rendered as a blank smudged placard, tattered striped tents and rigged wheels hazing into smoky black, ember sparks and sickly neon drifting through carnival-red gloom, a tender sinister transaction, rendered as soft pastel impressionism — hazy diffuse light, visible chalky texture, gentle gradients, dreamy muted color.
name: Free Game with Purchase
text:
As an additional cost to cast this spell, sacrifice an artifact or creature.
You draw two cards. The first one's free. The second one cost you something and you know it.
flavorText: There is no such thing as a free game. There is barely such a thing as a game.
